name: Metamachy
author: Jean-Louis Cazaux (2012) / AGP port
description: Jean-Louis Cazaux's flagship large chess variant (2012) on a 12x12 board: the orthodox army plus an Eagle (gryphon: one diagonal step then an outward orthogonal slide), a Lion (leaps to any square within two king steps), two Princes (non-royal kings with a pawn-like double push that promote on the last rank), two Camels ((1,3) leapers), two Elephants (ferz + alfil), and two Xiangqi Cannons (hop-capture over a screen). Pawns -- and Princes -- may make their non-capturing double step from ANY square, with en passant (by pawns only) anywhere on the board; promotion is to Queen, Eagle or Lion only. There is no castling: instead an unmoved King may once leap two squares in any of 16 directions (orthogonal, diagonal or knight-wise) over any piece, but not out of check and not over a threatened square. This port fixes the standard default array (Queen f1, King g1, Lion f2, Eagle g2); the real game lets Black permute those four pieces (12 setups). Checkmate wins; stalemate is a draw.
